mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, causing negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in greater than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE crosses the 5.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, generating positive medium-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects capacity constraints and margin pressure, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day returns in greater than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with falling unemployment creates stagflationary pressure that compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising FEDFUNDS and falling UNRATE yields positive 20-day returns in greater than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent annualized signals economic contraction that triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 60 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.5 percent annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive medium-term equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with restrictive policy rates forces central banks to maintain tight liquidity, suppressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The high-inflation tight-policy regime produces positive 20-day returns in greater than 50 percent of triggered months.
